76281.subject:
AI Ambition Is Cheap. Execution Is the Advantage; AI chatbot that
 took over 8,500 CS roles but no one got fired!
76281.body:
AI Frontier

Автор: Steve Nouri


Автор: Steve Nouri

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-ambition-cheap-execution-advantage-chatbot-took-over-steve-nouri-bxxoc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BPYBH58OzSF6Xf4HBV3vDLw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3R5z1Hlm7ySYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrus2fj0~nz-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrus2fj0-nz
  
AI Ambition Is Cheap. Execution Is the Advantage. Generative and agentic AI have made experimentation easier than ever, but launching pilots is not the same as creating business value. The real divide is now between companies that test AI and companies that can repeatedly build, deploy, adopt, and scale it. Most wining companies that I talked with concentrated their transformation on three or fewer priority areas. They did not spread AI across the organization. They focused resources where the economic impact was highest. That is the first lesson: stop collecting use cases. Many companies deploy copilots, agents, and automation tools inside existing processes. This may improve individual tasks, but it rarely changes the economics of the business. The larger opportunity is to redesign complete workflows. Instead of asking how AI can accelerate one step in claims processing, supply-chain planning, customer support, or software development, leaders should ask what the entire process would look like if it were rebuilt around AI. McKinsey found that reimagining workflows end to end had one of the strongest correlations with value. The second lesson is that AI transformation is a people transformation. Agents may take over routine execution, coordination, and some decision-making, but this changes human roles rather than removing the need for strong teams. Companies must redesign responsibilities, retrain employees, and combine human judgment with machine execution. The third lesson is speed. DBS once needed 18 months to put its first AI model into production. It can now deploy one every two months. The advantage is not any single model. It is the organizational system that allows the company to keep moving faster. Finally, AI cannot be delegated to the technology department. Successful transformation requires the CEO to make it a top priority, while business leaders, finance, HR, product, and technology teams share responsibility for delivery. AI becomes valuable when the business owns the outcome, not when IT owns another project. The companies winning with AI are not necessarily using the most advanced models. They are choosing fewer priorities, redesigning complete workflows, preparing people for adoption, and building the ability to move from idea to impact repeatedly. AI ambition gets attention. Execution creates advantage. Strugglin

76279.subject: Is PUMP actually cheap?
76279.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

———————————————————————————

Hi all, happy Tuesday! Crypto extended last week’s divergence on Monday, with BTC and most sectors moving higher even as equities softened. Crypto-linked equities led the move, while AI remained under pressure. 

Today, we also dive into PUMP: up 50% on the month, through its first unlock, and once again sparking talk that the trenches are back. We look at whether the fundamentals justify the move and what the market is actually pricing.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

Monday was broadly risk-on for crypto even as traditional markets softened. The 2025 Crypto Equity Cohort (+5.2%), DEXs (+2.7%), and Oracle (+2.4%) led the sector board, while Bittensor Ecosystem (-2.1%), AI (-1.4%), and Modular (-0.9%) lagged. BTC gained 1% as the S&P 500 fell -0.3%. If sustained, this would extend the divergence highlighted yesterday, when BTC was the only major benchmark to finish last week in the green.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/97f9e082-aeab-417a-bbc6-9f66bea8db6d/image.png?t=1784641255)
Caption: 

All five of the 2025 Crypto Equity Cohort constituents closed higher: GEMI (+6.0%), CRCL (+5.6%), FIGR (+4.7%), GLXY (+4.6%) and BLSH (+2.4%). The likely catalyst came from Washington, where [progress](https://x.com/sjdedic/status/2079472779615699032?s=20) on the CLARITY Act’s merged Senate text, including progress with the White House on ethics language, moved the bill closer to a potential floor vote before the August recess. Senate Majority Leader John Thune has targeted the week of July 20 for action. 

The bid extended to crypto treasury companies, including SBET (+8.5%), BTBT (+7.7%), BMNR (+6.6%) and MSTR (+4.6%). Part of the strength may reflect capital rotating out of crowded AI positions and into names that have significantly underperformed over the past year.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3dd762e9-f18b-419b-ab20-628c358cebd8/image.png?t=1784641255)
Caption: 

Beyond DEXs and Oracle, Perps (+2.3%), L2s (+2.2%) and DeFi (+2.1%) also outperformed, while Revenue Leaders gained 1.9%. We also saw the rotation back toward Solana continue, although momentum eased. Solana Eco gained 1.2%, while Launchpad slowed from 3.9% on Sunday to 0.7% on Monday as the PUMP rally moderated. 

The broadening of market leadership beyond last week’s PUMP-led move is constructive. Conti

76277.subject: 📈 Bitcoin surges past $66K
76277.body:
Today’s newsletter is brought to you by **[Choose Rich Live](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**[!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)

Our very own Nick O’Neill is has launched a new stream covering crypto, finance, AI, tech, and the stories shaping the internet economy.

If you already follow Nick, you know what to expect: sharp takes, market chaos, founder brain, and the occasional banger you’ll see clipped on the timeline five minutes later.

Episodes will be streaming live on YouTube, so make sure you’re subscribed to catch the next one as it happens.

**[Subscribe to Choose Rich Live!](https://www.youtube.com/@chooserichnick/streams)**

———————————————————————————

## Top Stories

**BTC** $66,415 (3.0%) | **ETH** $1,936 (3.7%) | **SOL** $78 (2.3%)

* Bitcoin surpasses [$66K ](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/bitcoin/)for the first time since June 15

* Bitmine [adds $14M](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2079184276592222602?s=20) worth of Ethereum as spot ETH ETFs led all crypto ETFs in [weekly net inflows](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2079068925783105812?s=20)

* Mediators are negotiating a [10 day cease fire](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2079261822121857132?s=20) between US and Iran

* Hyperliquid adds support for [HIP-4 prediction market creation](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2079056881440739826?s=20) to those with 500k staked HYPE

* Bitcoin miners Hut 8 and Iren [pivot to AI](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2079231844600643790?s=20) contracts

* Former NY Governor Andrew Cuomo joins [OKX’s board](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2079162109947240463?s=20) — NYSE partners with OKX to [tokenize stocks](https://x.com/coinbureau/status/2079295278843351548?s=20)

* Polymarket refers [100 suspicious wallets](https://x.com/WuBlockchain/status/2079389757231251823?s=20) to law enforcement

* Robinhood opens access to [AI agent accounts](https://x.com/RobinhoodApp/status/2079195918595764300?s=20)

* Google announces new [“Frozen” chip](https://x.com/KobeissiLetter/status/2079198142218588589?s=20) to power its AI models

———————————————————————————


———

You are reading a plain text version of this post. For the best experience, copy and paste this link in your browser to view the post online:
https://www.dailybones.com/p/bitcoin-surges-past-66k

76274.body: #outlook a { padding:0; } body { margin:0; padding:0; -webkit-text-size-adjust:100%; -ms-text-size-adjust:100%; } table, td { border-collapse:collapse; mso-table-lspace:0pt; mso-table-rspace:0pt; } img { border:0; height:auto; line-height:100%; outline:none; text-decoration:none; -ms-interpolation-mode:bicubic; } p { display:block; margin:13px 0; } 96 .mj-outlook-group-fix { width:100% !important; } @media only screen and (min-width:480px) { .mj-column-per-100 { width:100% !important; max-width: 100%; } .mj-column-per-25 { width:25% !important; max-width: 25%; } .mj-column-per-50 { width:50% !important; max-width: 50%; } } .moz-text-html .mj-column-per-100 { width:100% !important; max-width: 100%; } .moz-text-html .mj-column-per-25 { width:25% !important; max-width: 25%; } .moz-text-html .mj-column-per-50 { width:50% !important; max-width: 50%; } @media only screen and (max-width:480px) { table.mj-full-width-mobile { width: 100% !important; } td.mj-full-width-mobile { width: auto !important; } } :root { color-scheme: light !important; supported-color-schemes: light !important; } @media only screen and (min-width:480px) { body:not(.gjs-dashed) .hidden-desktop { display: none !important; } div.mj-group-full-width { width: 100% !important; max-width: 100% !important; } } @media only screen and (max-width:480px) { body:not(.gjs-dashed) .hidden-mobile { display: none !important; } } Повідомлення про конвертацію видалених токенів Шановний користувачу Binance!   Binance виконає конвертацію RDNT у USDC на основі балансу ваших активів в акаунті Binance о 2026-07-23 03:00:00 UTC . Конвертація буде завершена, і сума в еквіваленті USDC буде зарахована на ваш акаунт Binance до 2027-01-23 03:00:00 UTC .   Про конвертацію   Конвертація буде виконана на основі середнього обмінного курсу RDNT до USDC у період з 2026-07-23 03:00:00 UTC і 2027-01-23 03:00:00 UTC (далі — "Період конвертації"). Зверніть увагу, що Binance надішле окреме сповіщення всім користувачам, на яких вплине конвертація RDNT у USDC, після її завершення.   Додаткова інформація про середній курс обміну протягом періоду конвертації також буде надана в повідомленні.   Примітки: Ви отримали цей електронний лист, оскільки наразі на балансі вашого акаунту Binance є токени RDNT. Депозити RDNT більше не підтримуються і не будуть зараховані. Протягом періоду конвертації користувачі не зможуть переглядати баланси своїх токенів RDNT у своїх акаунтах Binance. Конвертація RDNT у USDC буде недоступна для користувачів, які знаходяться, зареєстровані або іншим чином перебувають в юрисдикціях, зазначених у Розділі (ii) Списку заборонених країн, визначеного в Умовах користування Binance. Цей електронний лист може бути перекладений іншою мовою, крім англійської. У разі невідповідності, двозначності або пропуску переважну силу матиме текст цього електронного листа англійською мовою.   Дякуємо за вашу підтримку! Команда Binance       Залишайтеся на зв’язку! 76273.subject: Why Your AI Agent Isn't Actually an Agent (And What's Missing)
76273.body:
Ment Tech | Meant To Innovate

Автор: Ment Tech Labs


Автор: Ment Tech Labs

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/why-your-ai-agent-isnt-actually-whats-missing-menttechlabs-rqloc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BKCExkP6JRFmQ3%2FqZ0MetGg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1WG_NE0oFlSYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrun1q95~wm-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrun1q95-wm
  
Open ten "AI agent" product pages this week. Nine of them are chatbots with a system prompt, a nice UI, and a name that ends in "AI". It answers a question, maybe calls one API, and stops. That's not an agent. That's autocomplete with a job title. As an AI agent development company, we build these systems for a living, so we see the gap up close: the distance between what gets demoed on a sales call and what actually survives a Tuesday morning in production, with real users, real edge cases, and a real API bill. Most of what's marketed today as AI agent development services is really chatbot development with a rebrand, and most of the AI agent platforms built on top of it inherit the same one-shot limitation. Here's what that gap actually looks like and what separates the two. The One-Shot Problem A real agent doesn't just respond. It decides what to do next. Most "agents" on the market today do a single pass: read input, generate output, and are done. No memory of the last 5 things the user asked about. No ability to say "I need more information before I answer that." No sense of whether the task actually got completed. A production agent runs a loop: plan, act, check the result, adjust, and act again until the task is actually finished, not until the model has produced a plausible-sounding response. That loop is the entire difference between a chatbot and an agent. Everything else is branding. Three Questions That Actually Separate the Two If you're evaluating an "AI agent", yours or a vendor's, these are the questions that matter more than any demo: 1. Does it remember? Not "Does it have a long context window?" Does it retain relevant state across sessions, so a returning user isn't starting from zero every time? Memory is what turns a tool into an assistant. 2. Can it actually do things, or does it just talk about them? Real tool access checking a database, sending an email, updating a CRM, calling an internal system is what makes an agent useful instead of conversational. This is where MCP (Model Context Protocol) has quietly become the standard worth paying attention to in 2026: it's the plumbing that lets an agent connect to your actual tools and data sources without a custom integration for every single one. If a vendor can't explain how their agent connects to your systems, it's a chat window, not an agent.
